paper_title: Pollen and Diatom Analysis in Estuarine Paleomorphology

methods.description: This method describes the collection and preparation of core samples for pollen and diatom analysis, including the elimination of contaminants and extraction of organic matter.

steps.description: Core data were collected using a percussion drilling Robotic Key System (RKS), model COBRA mk1.
steps.searchable_summary: collect core samples using COBRA mk1 drilling system

steps.description: Seventeen wood, charcoal, and organic sediments were dated by accelerator mass spectrometer (AMS) at the Beta Analytic Radiocarbon Dating Laboratory.
steps.searchable_summary: perform AMS radiocarbon dating on organic samples

steps.description: Possible contaminants, such as modern roots, were eliminated manually during the pre-treatment.
Organic matter from sediments was extracted according to the laboratory standard pretreatment with acid wash.
steps.searchable_summary: extract organic matter using acid wash

steps.description: Conventional 14C ages were calibrated to calendar years using the Pretoria Calibration Procedure program.

methods.description: This method outlines the extraction, identification, and analysis of pollen grains from core samples.

steps.description: Pollen extraction followed standard techniques with HCl, KOH, HF, and acetolysis.
steps.searchable_summary: extract pollen using HCl, KOH, HF, and acetolysis

steps.description: Pollen concentration in grains/cm3 was determined by adding one tablet of Lycopodium spores to each sample before treatment.
steps.searchable_summary: determine pollen concentration using Lycopodium spores

steps.description: At least 300 tree and herb grains were counted for each sample using a Carl Zeiss Axioskop 40 microscope.
Pollen grains were identified using reference collection and published catalogs.
steps.searchable_summary: identify pollen grains using reference materials

steps.description: Pollen sum, percentage, and concentration were calculated using TILIA and TILIAGRAPH.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate pollen statistics using TILIA and TILIAGRAPH

methods.description: This method describes the preparation, identification, and analysis of diatom samples from core samples.

steps.description: Preparation of diatom samples was based on 30% H2O2 and 10% HCl.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare diatom samples using H2O2 and HCl

steps.description: Diatoms were mounted in slides using Naphrax.
steps.searchable_summary: mount diatoms on slides with Naphrax

steps.description: Identification was based on the author's own reference collection and published diatom morphological descriptions.
steps.searchable_summary: identify diatoms using reference collection and descriptions

steps.description: A minimum and maximum of 200 and 500 valves, respectively, were counted for each slide.
Sum, percentage, and concentration calculations followed the same procedure adopted for pollen analysis.
